{{# @name Livewire collection — tours @desc Tours grid for /tours, rendered by reach/statamic-livewire-filters. Each entry uses the shared `components/tour-card` partial so the /tours grid and the homepage featured-tours carousel stay visually consistent. When the active filters return zero entries the grid is replaced by an inline empty state — "Reset filters" calls the LivewireCollection's `clearAll` method, which fires `clear-all-filters` to every filter component on the page. Animation lives at this level rather than via `x-reveal` because Livewire morphs the grid on every filter / sort change, and the once-only reveal directive can't handle that. Instead we hook the package's `filter-updated` / `sort-updated` / `entries-updated` events: cards fade out together (delay 0) when filters change, then fade back in with an index-based stagger when the new entries land. `loading` starts true so the very first paint also animates — initial load matches every filter change. The per-card stagger caps at index 8 so the cascade stays within the brand's <400ms standard-band budget (8 × 50ms = 400ms). Cards beyond index 8 share the 400ms delay and arrive together as a single batch behind the cascade — mostly below the fold on typical viewports, so the visual impact is minimal. Easing + duration mirror the `card` reveal preset (440ms / `--ease-standard`) so motion identity is consistent with the rest of the site's reveals. Reduced-motion users keep `loading: false` throughout, so the cards are always visible and filter changes happen instantly. #}}
{{ if entries }}
{{ entries }}
{{ partial:components/tour-card }}
{{ /entries }}
{{ links }}
{{ else }}
{{ svg src="icons/trekking-binoculars" attr:class="size-12 fill-current" attr:aria-hidden="true" attr:role="img" }}
{{ partial:typography/h3 content="No tours match those filters" color="text-foreground" class="!m-0 text-center" }}

Try changing or removing some of your filters.

{{ /if }}
